paper_title: Salinity, microbe and carbonate mineral relationships in brackish and hypersaline lake sediments: A case study from the tropical Pacific coral atoll of Kiritimati

methods.method_name: Organic and Inorganic Carbon and Mineral Analysis
methods.description: This method involves the preparation and analysis of sediment samples to estimate organic matter and carbonate content through combustion and to assess mineralogy using X-ray diffraction.

steps.description: Sediment subsamples were rinsed multiple times with deionized water to remove glutaraldehyde and pore water containing sulphates.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse sediment samples with deionized water to remove contaminants

steps.description: Rinsed sediment was dried at 120°C overnight.
Dried sediment was transferred to crucibles and weighed.
Samples were burned at 550°C for 2 hr in a muffle furnace to combust organic content.
steps.searchable_summary: combust organic content in sediment at 550°C

steps.description: Samples were burned at 1,050°C for 4 hr to combust carbonate.
Percent organic and calcium carbonate abundance were estimated based on mass measurements following each combustion.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percent organic and carbonate content from mass measurements
inputs: mass measurements before and after combustion

steps.description: Sediment mineralogy was assessed with powder X-ray diffraction on dried, pulverized sediment subsamples.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sediment mineralogy using X-ray diffraction

steps.description: Sediment mineral components were analysed with Jade+ 9.5 software.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze mineral components using Jade+ 9.5 software

steps.description: The Rietveld Refinement was applied to determine mineral phase percentages for each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Rietveld Refinement to determine mineral phase percentages

methods.description: This method describes the geographical context and procedures for collecting water and sediment samples from various sites on Kiritimati Island for analysis.

steps.description: Water and sediment/mat samples were obtained in Kiritimati in July and August of 2014.
steps.searchable_summary: collect water and sediment samples from Kiritimati

steps.description: Water was sampled from the lakes, the open ocean and the main lagoon at the depth of the sediment-water interface (20-50 cm) near shore, in the photic zone.
steps.searchable_summary: sample water from various aquatic environments at specific depths

steps.description: Temperature, specific conductance, pH, and dissolved oxygen content were measured in situ using a YSI ProPlus multiparameter water quality sonde.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water quality parameters in situ

steps.description: Salinity values were calculated using specific conductance values relative to 25°C.
A Hach test kit was used to measure in situ alkalinity.
steps.searchable_summary: measure alkalinity using Hach test kit

steps.description: Water samples were filtered through a 0.22 μm filter and stored in acid washed bottles for cation analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: filter and store water samples for cation analysis

steps.description: The concentration of major and minor cations was measured using an ICP-OES.
Nearshore sediment samples from the sediment/matwater interface to 5 cm depth were taken from all locations at 20-50 cm water depth.
steps.searchable_summary: collect nearshore sediment samples from specified depths

steps.description: The samples were placed in sterile Whirlpak bags, fixed with 3% glutaraldehyde in a phosphate buffer, homogenized and stored on ice.
steps.searchable_summary: fix and store sediment samples for preservation

steps.description: Subsamples intended for DNA analysis were frozen and stored at −80°C prior to DNA extraction and PCR amplification.
steps.searchable_summary: store subsamples for DNA analysis at −80°C
